ksa_text: Knowledge of data standardization policies and procedures, including regulatory standards (ICH E6, 21 CFR Part 11, Annex 11), industry standards (CDISC CDASH, SDTM, ADaM), and submission dataset requirements for regulatory agencies.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Quality is a primary or secondary domain for the VP Data Architecture. This knowledge KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (vp).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Data Quality), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Framework signal is weak (composite 0.405). External validation of this knowledge KSA for the VP Data Architecture role relies primarily on domain-level alignment rather than specific framework matches.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: DAMA-DMBOK Data Quality Management chapter and industry surveys confirm data quality competencies for quality-focused leadership roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: domain-level relevance of Data Quality for the VP Data Architecture is corroborated by DAMA-DMBOK, CDO survey data, and role-specific job market analysis, despite weak cross-framework signal.

decision_rationale: Data Quality knowledge KSA is within scope for the VP Data Architecture at the vp tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
